'''Joralla''' was a [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Mektrun sector/Legends|Mektrun sector]]. It [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Red giant/Legends|red giant]] [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Jaska]] and had an unstable climate that displayed considerable weather and seasonal changes. The planet's surface was divided between vast [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] and three main continents, whose terrain included high [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], [[Forest/Legends|forests]], and verdant jungles. The latter featured remarkably diverse [[Plant/Legends|plant]] life, including [[tequa bush]]es, [[sio tree]]s, [[redspar]] [[Fruit/Legends|fruits]], and [[kewafi flower]]s. Various animal [[Species/Legends|species]] also inhabited Joralla, such as the [[Diet/Legends|herbivorous]] [[oslet]]s and the predatory [[wulkarsk]]s.

The [[Tikiarri]] were a species of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Bird/Legends|bird]] that were tribal-based and highly aggressive. They frequently attempted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] off-worlders in order to acquire advanced [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weapons and were considered a menace in the neighboring [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. At some point long before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Joralla was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to another sentient species, the [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Wasilsi]]. They frequently engaged the Tikiarri in conflicts and eventually went extinct, leaving behind architectural ruins. The [[sentientologist]] [[Beyorth Gommdora]] theorized the Wasilsi originated off-planet and were eradicated by local [[Bacteria/Legends|bacteria]] or viruses. Joralla also occasionally received off-world visitors in the form of independent [[Merchant/Legends|traders]].

Around [[3643 BBY]], Joralla was placed under quarantine and [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] due to an outbreak of a plague. Later, access to the planet was once again restricted under the authority of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Debin Seylas]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] due to the nearby systems experiencing troubles with the unruly Tikiarri. During the Galactic Civil War, the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Adriav Kavos]] arranged to sell a [[Thief/Legends|stolen]] [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Datacard/Legends|data disk]] to a buyer on Joralla. A group of Rebel agents subsequently traveled to the planet in order to retrieve the data disk. The off-worlders were attacked by a tribe of Tikiarri before the buyer, an [[Unidentified Imperial spy (Joralla)|Imperial spy]] intent on double-crossing Kavos, arrived on Joralla as well.

==Description==
===Astrography and atmosphere===
Joralla was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Joralla system/Legends|Joralla system]],<ref name="TSWPC" /> a part of the [[Mektrun sector/Legends|Mektrun sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The primary planet in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|173|Joralla}}</ref> after which it was named, Joralla occupied the third and outermost [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position around its [[Star/Legends|sun]], the [[Red giant/Legends|red giant]]–type star [[Jaska]], which bathed the planet in [[Color/Legends|crimson]] light. Joralla had a rotational period of twenty-six [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]], and its [[local year]] lasted 310 [[Local day/Legends|local days]]. The planet had standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] and had no [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="TSWPC" />

The [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of Joralla was Type I, meaning it was well-suited for [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-breathing [[Species/Legends|species]]. The planet's climate was dependent on its geographic latitude, being [[Temperature/Legends|hot]] in the tropical and equatorial zones, cold in the northern and southern polar regions, and temperate in the areas situated between the two aforementioned regions. At the height of [[Summer/Legends|summer]] in the equatorial zones, the temperature could reach a peak of 50 degrees celsius, while in the polar regions, it could drop to a low of -35 degrees celsius during [[Winter/Legends|winter]]. Joralla's climate was also unstable, with abrupt changes from [[Standard month/Legends|month]] to month caused by considerable weather and seasonal fluctuations. In the planet's [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] regions, [[Water/Legends|rain]] occurred regularly.<ref name="TSWPC" />

===Geography===
[[File:Mektrun-EAOC.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Joralla was a jungle planet located in the Mid Rim's Mektrun sector.]]
The majority of Joralla's surface was covered by rich [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] of deep blue water. The planet's land area featured three main continents. Joralla's western hemisphere had a single continent situated along the planet's tropical climate band yet also reaching the northern polar region. The eastern hemisphere's landmass was divided into two continents that both lay mostly in the tropical band, with the extreme northern and southern areas reaching into the planet's temperate regions. Four islands were also located between the two eastern continents.<ref name="TSWPC" />

Joralla featured a diverse topography. The planet's main continents were covered with high [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] ranges that included both dormant and active [[Volcano/Legends|volcanoes]] as well as immense waterfalls that cascaded from the mountaintops. Unimaginably powerful [[Groundquake/Legends|earthquakes]] had ripped chasms into the planet's bedrock, and those openings were being pummeled by the waterfalls. Elsewhere, rivers wound atop plateaus that were hundreds of [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] in width. The planet had a moderate [[Hydrosphere/Legends|hydrosphere]]. Joralla also had potentially vast deposits of [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]],<ref name="TSWPC" /> and [[Jorallan opal]]s<ref name="CSWE opal" /> and [[Jorallan pearl]]s were two types of [[Gemstone/Legends|gemstones]] found on the planet, with the latter specifically originating in its oceans<ref name="CSWE pearls">{{CSWECite|II|173|Jorallan pearls}}</ref> and the former being among the valuables circulating in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="PP" />

A primarily arboreal planet, Joralla had vast [[Forest/Legends|forests]] located on the top of plateaus, and its continents also had cool forest regions. However, the planet's dominant terrain feature was lush, thick jungles that obscured the other landforms and were often too thick to traverse via [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]] and [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]]. The jungle planet was a paradise world that was both dangerous and displayed an uncommon, savage beauty.<ref name="TSWPC" />

===Flora and fauna===
Joralla's flora and fauna displayed remarkable diversity and beauty. The cooler forest regions of the planet were home to many similar species of [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal. In the jungles, the plant life was lush and colorful and featured thousands of [[Fern (plant)/Legends|fern]], [[Bush/Legends|bush]], and [[Tree/Legends|tree]] species. The latter two included the [[tequa bush]]es and the towering [[sio tree]]s. Vines, striking [[Flower/Legends|flowers]], and abundant amounts of [[Vegetable/Legends|vegetables]] and [[Fruit/Legends|fruits]] also grew on Joralla, with the giant [[redspar]] fruit found almost anywhere on the planet. Many plants on Joralla possessed defense mechanisms, such as the ability to shoot [[Poison/Legends|poisonous]] barbs, as exhibited by the [[kewafi flower]]s.<ref name="TSWPC" />

Joralla was home to a wealth of extremely varied animal species, specimens of which could have commanded high [[Currency/Legends|prices]] on other worlds. Jorallan animals, in combination with the local plant life, provided any visitors to the planet with a rich and varied [[Diet/Legends|diet]]. Many species of [[Insect/Legends|insect]], [[Mammal/Legends|mammal]], [[Bird/Legends|bird]], and [[Reptile/Legends|reptile]] hid among the verdant plant life. Different varieties of wild game animals were present on Joralla, such as the timid, six-[[Limb/Legends|limbed]] arboreal herbivores known as [[oslet]]s. The planet was also home to many predators. The [[wulkenso]] family included several closely related species of jungle and forest animals, of which the predatory [[wulkarsk]] was the largest member.<ref name="TSWPC" />

The [[sentientologist]] [[Beyorth Gommdora]] hypothesized that the [[Wasilsi]], a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species that at one point inhabited Joralla, were eradicated by a plague caused by local [[Bacteria/Legends|bacteria]] or viruses. Such microorganisms could have had survived on the planet until the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="TSWPC" />

==History==
===A quarantined world===
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
At some point around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="Date">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "Blockade Run" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the game, the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> a plague had broken out on Joralla. As a result, the planet was subjected to an official quarantine and [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] by the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]]. An individual affiliated with one of the sides in the [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] ongoing at that time between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the Sith Empire sent a companion to run the blockade and deliver much-needed medicine to Joralla's inhabitants in order to win their support. The companion [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggled]] the medicine past the blockade, and the highly grateful citizens of the planet rewarded the individual in return.<ref name="SWTOR">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Underworld Trading|Blockade Run}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the star systems neighboring the Joralla system experienced problems with containing the unruly and aggressive nature of the Jorallan-native [[Tikiarri]]. On many nearby planets, any [[Starship/Legends|starship]] crew that mentioned it had visited Joralla had its vessel promptly searched for any members of the bothersome species, who were then either [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] or [[Death/Legends|executed]]. Since the Empire refused to officially quarantine Joralla, Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Debin Seylas]] had to appease the local systems by placing the planet under an unofficial quarantine and posting a regular patrol of the system, carried out by a pair of [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat]]s.<ref name="TSWPC" />

===A rendezvous on Joralla===
[[Adriav Kavos]], the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the [[440 freighter|freighter]] ''[[Isilia]]'', [[Thief/Legends|stole]] an important [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Datacard/Legends|data disk]] while attacking a group of Rebel agents in the<ref name="TSWPC" /> Mektrun<ref name="EAOC" /> [[Sector/Legends|sector]] during the Galactic Civil War. Kavos agreed to meet on Joralla an interested buyer—in reality, an [[Unidentified Imperial spy (Joralla)|Imperial spy]] planning not only to acquire the data disk, which was hidden in the modified [[Stock/Legends|stock]] of a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], but also to capture the ''Isilia''. The [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] traveled to the Joralla system without incident<ref name="TSWPC" /> but was forced to land<ref name="CSWE Isilia">{{CSWECite|II|119|''Isilia''}}</ref> in a clearing in the Jorallan jungle adjacent to a ruined Wasilsi temple.<ref name="TSWPC" />

{{RPGseed}}
[[File:TikiarriAttack.png|thumb|right|250px|A group of Tikiarri attack off-worlders in a ruined Wasilsi temple.]]
The Alliance sent a group of individuals to retrieve the data disk from Kavos. The Rebel agents arrived in the Joralla system late in the landing site's afternoon and, upon establishing contact with the ''Isilia''{{'s}} crew, posed as independent [[Merchant/Legends|traders]], allowing them to establish initially friendly relations. Late in the evening, a tribe of the local Tikiarri attacked the off-worlders, attempting to capture their [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] weaponry. The Imperial [[Spy/Legends|spy]] arrived early in the following morning, bringing along a complement of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] and summoning the Imperial Skipray Blastboats into the system.<ref name="TSWPC" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
[[File:Tikiarri-AlienEncounters.png|thumb|left|250px|The Tikiarri were a sentient species native to Joralla.]]
The Tikiarri were a sentient species of flying, carrion-eating bird native to Joralla. An estimated 10 million of the highly aggressive Tikiarri lived in unorganized tribes in the jungles of their homeworld. Ever since a careless trader introduced the Tikiarri to blaster technology, the natives made frequent attempts to acquire advanced weaponry by killing off-worlders. The dangerous nature of the avians led to an unofficial Imperial quarantine of their homeworld and its star system.<ref name="TSWPC" />

At some point long before the Galactic Civil War, Joralla was inhabited by another sentient species, the [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] Wasilsi. A religious people, the Wasilsi built permanent structures on the planet, utilized metalworking and a [[Wasilsi language|written language]], and created numerous works of art. For many [[Standard year/Legends|years]], the Wasilsi were engaged in conflicts with the Tikiarri until the former eventually went extinct. The sentientologist Beyorth Gommdora theorized that the Wasilsi originated off-planet, either as [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] or the survivors of a starship crash-landing on Joralla.<ref name="TSWPC" />

In addition to the native species, Joralla was occasionally visited by freighters of independent traders, despite the planet being nearly worthless economically. Those visitors resulted in a permanent planetary presence of no more than twenty [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="TSWPC" />

==Locations==
[[File:WasilsiTemple.png|thumb|right|250px|One of the Wasilsi temples on Joralla]]
The extinct Wasilsi species inhabited villages and used religious temples, the ruins of which had become buried in Joralla's soil or overgrown by plants by the time of the Galactic Civil War. The remains of those structures featured artwork on walls that depicted battles between the Wasilsi and the Tikiarri.<ref name="TSWPC" />

One of the Wasilsi temples was an octagonal structure located in the Jorallan jungle. In its center, the structure featured a domed space that had become exposed to the open sky upon the eventual degradation of the roof. The partial remains of a column, along with supports for torches, was located in the middle of the area. A fountain and a clearing with a landing field lay adjacent to the temple, as did the encampment of the freighter ''Isilia''{{'s}} crew.<ref name="TSWPC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Joralla was first mentioned in ''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]'', a [[1991]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The book presents an idea for a [[roleplaying game]] adventure that is set on Joralla. During the adventure, if the Tikiarri force is successful in its attack on the off-worlders, the natives can capture the blaster [[Rifle/Legends|rifle]] with the data disk inside, further complicating the player characters' mission. This article assumes the suggested scenario plays out as described.<ref name="PoG1">''[[Planets of the Galaxy, Volume One]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Joralla system, and therefore Joralla itself, in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" /> Joralla was subsequently mentioned in an Underworld Trading Crew Skill mission of the [[2011]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', though the information regarding the mission's outcome was later removed from the game.<ref name="SWTOR" />
